metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either gate this story on the community-health story delivering `SECURITY.md` first (add an explicit dependency), or omit the `Security` URL from `[project.urls]` in this story and add it as a follow-on once `SECURITY.md` exists.
findings.finding: SECURITY.md does not exist anywhere in the repository, yet the plan's AC specifies `[project.urls] Security -> SECURITY.md`. The plan defers its creation to a separate 'community-health story', making the Security URL a broken link at publish time if this story ships first.
findings.location: What / [project.urls] bullet; AC line: `[project.urls]` includes … Security
findings.suggested_fix: Add a story dependency on the community-health story in the ticket, or conditionally omit the Security key: add it only in a follow-up commit that also adds SECURITY.md, keeping the two changes atomic.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Guard the `Security` URL against its dependency: either (a) make this story explicitly depend on the community-health story that delivers `SECURITY.md`, or (b) omit the `Security` URL for now and add it in the community-health story itself, or (c) add a note that the AC for this story includes verifying `SECURITY.md` exists before merge.
findings.finding: The `Security` project URL points at `SECURITY.md`, which does not exist in the repository. The plan defers its creation to 'the community-health story' but imposes no ordering constraint, so this story could ship a broken PyPI sidebar link permanently.
findings.location: What / [project.urls] — `Security` entry; Scope paragraph ('The `Security` URL points at the file delivered by the community-health story')
findings.suggested_fix: Move the `Security = 'SECURITY.md'` URL entry into the community-health story that creates `SECURITY.md`, so both the file and its URL land atomically. Remove it from this story's scope.
Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either add `SECURITY.md` as part of this story (not deferred), or make the `Security` URL entry in `[project.urls]` conditional on the community-health story landing first and add an explicit dependency/ordering note so the pyproject.toml change is not merged before the file exists.
findings.finding: The plan's AC mandates a `Security` entry in `[project.urls]` pointing at `SECURITY.md`, but no `SECURITY.md` file exists anywhere in the repository. The plan defers creation of this file to a separate 'community-health story' without establishing a dependency order, meaning the URL would reference a nonexistent file at implementation time.
findings.location: ## What / [project.urls] bullet; ## Scope 'The Security URL points at the file delivered by the community-health story'; AC line: '[project.urls] includes … Security'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a minimal `SECURITY.md` stub (e.g., pointing to the issue tracker for vulnerability reports) in the same PR as the pyproject.toml metadata change, removing the dependency on the separate community-health story.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the macOS classifier from `Operating System :: MacOS` to `Operating System :: MacOS :: MacOS X` to match the valid PyPI trove classifier taxonomy.
findings.finding: The classifier `Operating System :: MacOS` is not a valid PyPI trove classifier. The correct form is `Operating System :: MacOS :: MacOS X`. An invalid classifier string is silently dropped by PyPI's display and will cause `twine check` (or `pyproject-validator`) to warn or fail.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace `Operating System :: MacOS` with `Operating System :: MacOS :: MacOS X` in the classifiers list.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete metadata-inspection command to the final AC (e.g. `python -m build && python -c "import importlib.metadata; m=importlib.metadata.metadata('<pkg>'); print(m['Author-email'], m['Keywords'])"` or `twine check dist/*` plus `tar -xOf dist/*.tar.gz '*/PKG-INFO'`) so 'rendered metadata shows the new fields' is deterministically verifiable.
findings.finding: The AC's proving command for metadata inspection — '`python -m build` (or the packaging metadata check) succeeds and the rendered metadata shows the new fields' — does not specify HOW to confirm the new fields appear in the built artifact, leaving 'rendered metadata shows the new fields' unverifiable without a concrete inspection command.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — last bullet ('`python -m build` (or the packaging metadata check) succeeds...')
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the vague clause with: `python -m build && tar -xOf dist/*.tar.gz '*/PKG-INFO' | grep -E '^(Author-email|Maintainer-email|Keywords|Classifier|Project-URL):'` — this produces explicit evidence that every new field was parsed and embedded by the build backend.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the `Documentation` URL as a full absolute GitHub URL (e.g., `https://github.com/navapbc/rebar/tree/main/docs`) rather than the ambiguous 'the repo `docs/`' shorthand.
findings.finding: The `Documentation` URL is described as pointing to 'the repo `docs/`', which is a directory path — not a valid web URL. PyPI project URLs must be absolute web URLs. An implementer reading this literally could write a relative path; even a well-intentioned implementer may not know the intended GitHub URL form.
findings.suggested_fix: Change the plan's Documentation entry to: `Documentation = 'https://github.com/navapbc/rebar/tree/main/docs'`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either add `build>=1.0` to the `dev` extra in `pyproject.toml` so `python -m build` is available after a standard `pip install -e '.[dev]'`, or replace the AC4 verification command with the already-available hatchling equivalent (`python -m hatchling build` / `hatch build`) that requires no new dependency.
findings.finding: `python -m build` is named in AC4 as the primary verification command, but the `build` PyPA frontend package is not declared in any dev dependency group in `pyproject.toml`, meaning it will be absent from a clean `pip install -e '.[dev]'` environment.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC4: "`python -m build` (or the packaging metadata check) succeeds"
findings.suggested_fix: Add `"build>=1.0"` to the `dev` extra list in `[project.optional-dependencies]`, or replace the AC4 check with `python -m hatchling build --hooks-only` (metadata-only, no wheel/sdist) which uses the already-declared `hatchling` backend without requiring the separate `build` frontend.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
